name: Hydroponics Cycle Manager
info:
## Purpose

Maintains the state of an active outside hydroponics control cycle.

The Cycle Manager groups multiple hardware events into a single logical
operation and determines whether the completed operation represents an:

• Auto Fill - Auto Dose cycle
• Manual Fill - Auto Dose cycle
• ESP-Override dose cycle

## Responsibilities

• Creates a new cycle when the first device starts.
• Tracks whether water fill, Pump A, and Pump B participated.
• Captures the control mode at cycle start.
• Preserves a single pending cycle object throughout the operation.
• Initializes synchronized A0 and A1 raw and filtered voltage fields.
• Generates capture requests for:
  - Before-cycle measurements
  - Fill completion
  - Dose A completion
  - Dose B completion
• Classifies the final event as FILL or DOSE.
• Provides the pending cycle to downstream logging and notification nodes.

## Conductivity Voltage Fields

Each new cycle initializes synchronized before-and-after voltage fields for
both ADS1115 conductivity channels.

A0 — KEYESTUDIO production/control channel:

• a0_raw_v_before
• a0_filtered_v_before
• a0_raw_v_after
• a0_filtered_v_after

A1 — DFRobot validation/comparison channel:

• a1_raw_v_before
• a1_filtered_v_before
• a1_raw_v_after
• a1_filtered_v_after

These fields store conductivity sensor voltages. They do not directly store
TDS or EC measurements.

## Revision History

### 2026-07-21 GAL

• Replaced the legacy tds_before and tds_after cycle fields.
• Added separate A0 and A1 raw and filtered voltage fields.
• Added before-and-after fields for synchronized conductivity snapshots.
• Renamed the delay_tds_capture routing property to
delay_post_mix_capture while preserving all cycle completion behavior.
• Preserved controller-mode notification title selection.

### 2026-07-10 GAL

• ISSUE-006 logging isolation work started.
• Added delay_tds_capture to each cycle:
  - Auto and Manual cycles use delayed post-mixing conductivity capture.
  - ESP-Override cycles are marked for immediate logging.
• Added msg.immediate_log on completed ESP-Override actions.
• Added msg.finalize_cycle to determine when a completed hardware action
  should be snapshotted and released:
  - Every ESP-Override action finalizes immediately.
  - Auto and Manual cycles finalize when Pump B completes.
• Establishes separate handling for independent ESP-Override fill,
  Pump A, and Pump B events without forcing them into one grouped cycle.

### 2026-07-06 GAL

• Updated controller operating mode terminology:
  - Auto Fill - Auto Dose
  - Manual Fill - Auto Dose
  - ESP-Override
• normalizeMode() updated to recognize the new Home Assistant
  control mode names while retaining backward compatibility
  with the legacy "Auto" and "Manual" values.
• Notification titles are selected using both the controller
  operating mode and completed event type (FILL or DOSE),
  allowing accurate user-facing notifications for each cycle.

### 2026-07-05 GAL

• Introduced unified pending cycle object.
• FILL cycles take precedence when water and dosing occur
  during the same operation.
• Captures HA control mode at cycle start instead of using
  hardcoded values.
• Added before/fill/dose capture flags for downstream
  processing.

### 2026-06-30 GAL

• Initial implementation.

Save After Values to Cycle
Build Cycle INSERT SQL
info:
# Build Cycle INSERT SQL

## Purpose

Builds the MariaDB `INSERT` statement for one completed hydroponics fill or maintenance-dose cycle.

The node uses the isolated completed-cycle snapshot carried in `msg.completed_cycle` rather than the shared `flow.outside_pending_cycle` object.

This allows a completed cycle to continue through its post-mixing measurement and logging sequence while a newer physical fill or dose cycle begins independently.

## Conductivity Channels

The completed cycle stores synchronized before-and-after voltage measurements for both conductivity channels:

- **A0 — KEYESTUDIO**
  - Production/control conductivity channel
- **A1 — DFRobot**
  - Validation and comparison conductivity channel

For each channel, both the raw ADS1115 measurement and the Home Assistant filtered measurement are recorded.

These values are analog voltages. They are not EC or TDS measurements.

## Recorded Engineering Data

- Controller operating mode
- Event type:
  - `FILL`
  - `DOSE`
- System gallons
- Water added
- Nutrient A dose
- Nutrient B dose
- A0 raw voltage before and after
- A0 filtered voltage before and after
- A1 raw voltage before and after
- A1 filtered voltage before and after
- Human-readable event summary
- Complete JSON engineering snapshot

## Voltage Field Mapping

| Measurement | Completed-cycle property | Database column |
|---|---|---|
| A0 raw before | `a0_raw_v_before` | `a0_raw_v_before` |
| A0 filtered before | `a0_filtered_v_before` | `a0_filtered_v_before` |
| A1 raw before | `a1_raw_v_before` | `a1_raw_v_before` |
| A1 filtered before | `a1_filtered_v_before` | `a1_filtered_v_before` |
| A0 raw after | `a0_raw_v_after` | `a0_raw_v_after` |
| A0 filtered after | `a0_filtered_v_after` | `a0_filtered_v_after` |
| A1 raw after | `a1_raw_v_after` | `a1_raw_v_after` |
| A1 filtered after | `a1_filtered_v_after` | `a1_filtered_v_after` |

## Human-Readable Summary

The `note` field provides a concise operator-facing summary containing:

- Operating mode
- Event type
- Water added
- Nutrient quantities
- System volume
- A0 filtered voltage before and after
- A1 filtered voltage before and after

Filtered voltages are used in the summary because they are more stable for operator review.

Raw and filtered measurements remain available in their dedicated database columns and in the complete JSON snapshot.

## Missing Values

Missing or invalid optional engineering values are written as SQL `NULL`.

They are not converted to zero because zero could represent a valid physical measurement and would incorrectly alter the historical record.

## Cycle Isolation

The node reads from:

`msg.completed_cycle`

It does not read from:

`flow.outside_pending_cycle`

This prevents a newly started physical cycle from modifying the measurements belonging to a previously completed cycle that is still waiting for its post-mixing capture.

## Outputs

- `msg.topic` contains the MariaDB `INSERT` statement.
- `msg.payload` contains the same SQL statement for the MariaDB node.

## Revision History

### 2026-07-21 — GAL

- Replaced legacy `tds_before`, `tds_after`, `a1_voltage_before`, and `a1_voltage_after` fields.
- Added separate A0/A1 raw and filtered before-and-after measurements.
- Updated the event summary to identify filtered voltage values correctly.
- Preserved all measurements in the complete JSON engineering snapshot.

### 2026-07-20 — GAL

- Added dual conductivity-channel logging.
- Expanded the engineering snapshot for channel comparison and Estimated EC model development.

### 2026-07-10 — GAL

- Implemented ISSUE-006 completed-cycle isolation.
- Changed the logging source from `flow.outside_pending_cycle` to `msg.completed_cycle`.
- Prevented overlapping maintenance cycles from being merged into one `maintenance_log` record.

name: Clear Pending Cycle
info:
Purpose

Finalizes the delayed logging and notification path for one completed
hydroponics cycle.

The shared flow.outside_pending_cycle slot is released earlier by the
Snapshot Completed Cycle node.

This node must not clear shared cycle state because a newer cycle may
already be active by the time the 10-minute mixing delay completes.

Revision History

2026-07-10  GAL
• ISSUE-006 logging isolation fix.
• Removed clearing of flow.outside_pending_cycle and
  flow.outside_cycle_active from this delayed node.
• Prevents an older completed cycle from clearing a newer active cycle.

name: Build Notification Text
info:
# Build Cycle Completion Notification

## Purpose

Builds the user-facing completion notification for one completed hydroponics fill or maintenance-dose cycle.

The node uses the isolated cycle snapshot carried in `msg.completed_cycle`.

This prevents a later physical cycle from replacing or modifying the measurements belonging to a completed cycle while it waits for post-mixing capture and notification.

## Supported Cycles

- Auto Fill - Auto Dose
- Manual Fill - Auto Dose
- Auto Maintenance Dose
- ESP-Override Fill
- ESP-Override Dose

## Notification Content

The notification displays only information relevant to the completed event.

### Fill Cycles

- Water added
- Nutrient A dose, when present
- Nutrient B dose, when present
- Starting reservoir volume
- Calculated final reservoir volume
- A0 filtered voltage before and after

### Dose Cycles

- Nutrient A dose
- Nutrient B dose
- System volume
- A0 filtered voltage before and after

## Conductivity Display

The notification uses:

`a0_filtered_v_before`

and:

`a0_filtered_v_after`

A0 is the KEYESTUDIO production/control conductivity channel.

The filtered value is used because it provides the most useful concise operator-facing indication of the reservoir change.

The notification labels the measurement as **A0 Filtered** because it is an analog voltage, not a TDS measurement.

Raw A0 values and both A1 values remain available in the database and complete engineering snapshot.

## Cycle Isolation

The node reads from:

`msg.completed_cycle`

It does not read from:

`flow.outside_pending_cycle`

This allows newer fill or dose activity to begin without altering the notification data for a previously completed cycle.

## Outputs

- `msg.title` contains the notification title.
- `msg.payload` contains the multi-line notification message.

## Revision History

### 2026-07-21 — GAL

- Replaced legacy `tds_before` and `tds_after` references.
- Changed the operator-facing conductivity label from TDS to A0 Filtered.
- Preserved completed-cycle isolation.

### 2026-07-10 — GAL

- Implemented ISSUE-006 notification isolation.
- Changed the notification source from `flow.outside_pending_cycle` to `msg.completed_cycle`.
- Prevented newer maintenance cycles from replacing earlier notification data.

### 2026-07-06 — GAL

- Updated notification terminology for the current controller operating modes.
- Selected notification titles using both operating mode and event type.

### 2026-07-05 — GAL

- Distinguished starting volume from calculated final fill volume.

### 2026-06-30 — GAL

- Initial implementation.

name: Notify Cycle Complete
info:
Purpose

Sends the final user notification after a hydroponics control cycle
has completed.

The notification title is supplied by `msg.title`, and the notification
body is supplied by `msg.payload` from the Build Notification Text
function.

This node does not determine notification content; it only delivers it.

Revision History

2026-07-06  GAL
• Renamed node to "Notify Cycle Complete."
• Changed notification title from a hardcoded value to `msg.title`.
• Supports dynamic titles for Auto Fill - Auto Dose,
  Manual Fill - Auto Dose, and ESP-Override cycles.

2026-06-30  GAL
• Initial implementation.

name: Snapshot Completed Cycle
info:
Purpose

Creates an independent snapshot of one completed physical hydroponics
fill or dose event.

The snapshot is carried downstream in msg.completed_cycle.

The shared flow.outside_pending_cycle slot is immediately released so a
later hardware event starts as a separate cycle instead of joining the
previous event.

Automatic and Manual cycles may continue through the 10-minute
post-mixing TDS path.

ESP-Override events may bypass the mixing delay and proceed directly
to database logging.

Revision History

2026-07-10  GAL
• ISSUE-006 logging isolation fix.
• Added independent completed-cycle snapshot.
• Releases flow.outside_pending_cycle immediately after cycle completion.
• Supports both delayed automatic-cycle logging and immediate
  ESP-Override event logging.
• Prevents consecutive physical events from being merged into one
  maintenance_log record.
